premise: If a credit card is not activated within 30 days of issuance, the issuer must seek OTP-based consent; absent consent, the account must be closed without cost within 7 working days of seeking confirmation.
hypothesis: If a credit card is not activated within 30 days of issuance, the issuer must seek OTP-based consent;
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: For an unsolicited card issued/activated and billed, the issuer must reverse the charges and pay the recipient a penalty of twice the value of the charges reversed.
hypothesis: For an unsolicited card issued/activated and billed, the issuer must reverse the charges and pay the recipient a penalty of twice the value of the charges reversed.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: The sanctioned credit limit shall not be breached at any point in time without explicit consent from the cardholder.
hypothesis: The sanctioned credit limit shall not be breached at any point in time without explicit consent from the cardholder.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues); failure incurs a penalty of Rs 500 per calendar day of delay until closure, provided no outstanding.
hypothesis: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues);
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: If a card is unused for more than one year, initiate closure after intimating the cardholder; if no reply within 30 days, close the account (subject to dues) and update the CIC within 30 days.
hypothesis: If a card is unused for more than one year, initiate closure after intimating the cardholder;
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: Minimum Amount Due must be stipulated so there is no negative amortization; unpaid charges, levies and taxes shall not be capitalized for charging or compounding of interest.
hypothesis: Minimum Amount Due must be stipulated so there is no negative amortization;
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: A card account may be reported past-due to CICs or levied penal charges only when past due for more than three days; days-past-due and late charges are computed from the payment due date; late charges apply only to the outstanding amount after the due date, not the Total Amount Due.
hypothesis: A card account may be reported past-due to CICs or levied penal charges only when past due for more than three days;
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: Interest shall be levied only on the outstanding amount, adjusted for payments, refunds and reversed transactions.
hypothesis: Interest shall be levied only on the outstanding amount, adjusted for payments, refunds and reversed transactions.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: Changes in charges take prospective effect only, with prior notice of at least one month.
hypothesis: Changes in charges take prospective effect only, with prior notice of at least one month.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: Bills must be sent without delay and the customer must have at least one fortnight for payment before interest begins to be charged.
hypothesis: Bills must be sent without delay and the customer must have at least one fortnight for payment before interest begins to be charged.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: If a cardholder protests a bill, the issuer must provide an explanation (with documentary evidence where applicable) within a maximum of 30 days from the date of complaint.
hypothesis: If a cardholder protests a bill, the issuer must provide an explanation (with documentary evidence where applicable) within a maximum of 30 days from the date of complaint.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: Explicit consent is required to adjust credit (from refund/failed/reversed transactions already paid for) beyond a cut-off of one percent of the credit limit or Rs 5000, whichever is lower; consent sought within 7 days of the credit; on cardholder request, reversal to the bank account within 3 working days.
hypothesis: Explicit consent is required to adjust credit (from refund/failed/reversed transactions already paid for) beyond a cut-off of one percent of the credit limit or Rs 5000, whichever is lower;
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: Total Amount Due is the total amount (net of credit received during the billing cycle, if any) payable by the cardholder as per the statement generated at the end of a billing cycle.
hypothesis: Total Amount Due is the total amount (net of credit received during the billing cycle, if any) payable by the cardholder as per the statement generated at the end of a billing cycle.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: If a complainant does not get a satisfactory response within a maximum period of 30 days from lodging the complaint, they may approach the RBI Ombudsman.
hypothesis: If a complainant does not get a satisfactory response within a maximum period of 30 days from lodging the complaint, they may approach the RBI Ombudsman.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: If a cardholder settles dues after being reported as a defaulter, the issuer must update the status with the CIC within 30 days from the date of settlement; default disclosure only after disputes are settled.
hypothesis: If a cardholder settles dues after being reported as a defaulter, the issuer must update the status with the CIC within 30 days from the date of settlement;
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: The instructions/Directions on KYC/AML/CFT issued by RBI from time to time shall be strictly adhered to in respect of all cards issued, including co-branded cards.
hypothesis: The instructions/Directions on KYC/AML/CFT issued by RBI from time to time shall be strictly adhered to in respect of all cards issued, including co-branded cards.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: Periodic updation of KYC shall be carried out at least once every two years for high-risk, once every eight years for medium-risk, and once every ten years for low-risk customers, from the date of account opening or last KYC updation.
hypothesis: Periodic updation of KYC shall be carried out at least once every two years for high-risk, once every eight years for medium-risk, and once every ten years for low-risk customers, from the date of account opening or last KYC updation.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: For partnership firms, the beneficial owner is the natural person with ownership of/entitlement to more than 10 percent of capital or profits (lowered from 15 percent by the 2023-10-17 amendment to the 2016 MD).
hypothesis: For partnership firms, the beneficial owner is the natural person with ownership of/entitlement to more than 10 percent of capital or profits (lowered from 15 percent by the 2023-10-17 amendment to the 2016 MD).

premise: Regulated entities must update KYC records in the Central KYC Records Registry within 7 days of receiving updated information from a customer.
hypothesis: Regulated entities must update KYC records in the Central KYC Records Registry within 7 days of receiving updated information from a customer.
design_intent: entailed (leading clause of the same regulatory text)

premise: 'Officially Valid Document (OVD)' means the passport, the driving licence, proof of possession of Aadhaar number, the Voter's Identity Card issued by the Election Commission of India, the job card issued by NREGA duly signed by an officer of the State Government, and the letter issued by the National Population Register containing details of name and address.
hypothesis: 'Officially Valid Document (OVD)' means the passport, the driving licence, proof of possession of Aadhaar number, the Voter's Identity Card issued by the Election Commission of India, the job card issued by NREGA duly signed by an officer of the State Government, and the letter issued by the National Population Register containing details of name and address.
